title: Validation, Release, Documentation, Recovery, and Portfolio Defense · Product Forensics
summary: A preserved decision record for validation, release, documentation, recovery, and portfolio defense contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in released product evidence and public defense packet and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pra…
objectives: Run task-based validation against predeclared outcomes and guardrails, analyze success, breakdown, accessibility, and contradictory evidence, then revise.
Bind source revision, dependencies, generated assets, build, SBOM or inventory, test evidence, deployment, and rollback to one release identity.
Define health, logs, metrics, alerts, support, backup, restore, incident, rollback, ownership, and retirement behavior proportionate to risk.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug User Validation
steps.instruction:
debug case — A polished developer-machine demo has no immutable artifact identity, clean setup, user-outcome evidence, restored backup, explicit limit, or unseen defense. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Run task-based validation against predeclared outcomes and guardrails, analyze success, breakdown, accessibility, and contradictory evidence, then revise.

Inspect the User Validation trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug User Validation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for run task-based validation against predeclared outcomes and guardrails, analyze success, breakdown, accessibility, and contradictory evidence, then revise. For Inspect the failure record · Debug User Validation, keep User Validation connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For User Validation, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug User Validation, name the types, ownership, and stop path, User Validation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug User Validation, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A polished developer-machine demo has no immutable artifact identity, clean setup, user-outcome evidence, restored backup, explicit limit, or unseen defense. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Run task-based validation against predeclared outcomes and guardrails, analyze success, breakdown, accessibility, and contradictory evidence, then revise.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug User Validation, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A satisfaction rating proves task success and user benefit.

stimulus.title: User Validation case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A polished developer-machine demo has no immutable artifact identity, clean setup, user-outcome evidence, restored backup, explicit limit, or unseen defense. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Run task-based validation against predeclared outcomes and guardrails, analyze success, breakdown, accessibility, and contradictory evidence, then revise.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A satisfaction rating proves task success and user benefit.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Run task-based validation against predeclared outcomes and guardrails, analyze success, breakdown, accessibility, and contradictory evidence, then revise. Artifact under review: released product evidence and public defense packet.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the inspect the failure record evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies User Validation, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Claim Defense Retro
steps.instruction:
Append a pp1-claim-defense-retro-hb1et: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Claim Defense Retro.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Claim Defense Retro into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Claim Defense Retro, keep Claim Defense Retro connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.code:
pp1-claim-defense-retro-hb1et:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Claim Defense Retro
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Claim Defense Retro: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A polished developer-machine demo has no immutable artifact identity, clean setup, user-outcome evidence, restored backup, explicit limit, or unseen defense. The debug variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Defend each mastery and impact claim with traceable evidence, answer counterexamples honestly, reflect on decisions, and define the next changed-context test.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug Claim Defense Retro.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug Claim Defense Retro, keep Claim Defense Retro connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend each mastery and impact claim with traceable evidence, answer counterexamples honestly, reflect on decisions, and define the next changed-context test.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug Claim Defense Retro, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Claim Defense Retro precondition, or invariant that must hold.
